title: Pre-process transcript to escape/strip backticks before the FM call (fixes truncation)
description: digikoma-recap's first proving run against .tmp/recap-fixtures/sample-session.txt surfaced string-field truncation in any RecapModel field that quoted backticked transcript content. Specifically: bugsCaught[0].description ended at literal 'Pi`s `composition: "' (truncated), and arcs[1].narrative ended at 'missing `identity.profileType: "' (truncated). Diagnosis: @Generable String boundary interacts badly with source content containing backticks (likely a JSON-quoting issue inside the FM output channel). Fix candidates: (a) pre-strip or escape backticks in the transcript before passing to LanguageModelSession.respond, OR (b) add a parallel `quotes: [String]` companion field on each list type for verbatim citations while keeping the human-readable narrative free of backticks. Option (a) is the smaller change; (b) is more honest about the structure. Verify with a re-run against the same fixture and a backtick-free expected output.
resolutionNote: Landed: in DigikomaRecapTool.swift, added a backtick-strip pre-processing step before the LanguageModelSession.respond call. Replaces ``` with ''' and single ` with single quote. Re-run against .tmp/recap-fixtures/sample-session.txt produced sample-session.v2.recap.json with zero truncated strings; all 3 fixture bugs surfaced cleanly with single-quote-escaped backtick content (e.g., 'Pi's composition: harness is invalid').
